input.background: A $t$ test calculates $t_{\text {stat }}=6.60$. Assuming the study had more than just a few observations, you do not need a $t$ table or software utility to draw a conclusion about the test.
input.question: What is the conclusion about the test?

output.answer: When the sample contains more than just a few observations, the associated $t$ statistic will have more than a few $d f$ and will look very much like a Standard Normal 2 distribution. Based on the 68-95-99.7 rule, we would almost never get a test statistic that is 6.6 standard deviations away from the 0.
